name: Inverse Galois Theorem

phase_0.boundary_condition: The Inverse Galois Theorem: every finite group is realizable as the Galois group of some field extension — the closure of all possible group-to-extension mappings.

VINIT.chosen_element: The abstract finite group G (unspecified, unnamed)
VINIT.justification: Before any field extension is constructed, G exists only as a potential, void of realization. This is the seed state — the group as pure algebraic object without instantiation.

TANCH.chosen_element: The universal field ℚ̄ (algebraic closure of ℚ, or any base field)
TANCH.justification: The hermetic vessel that contains all possible field extensions and their Galois groups. It is the terminal anchor — the boundary within which all realizations must occur.

AFWD.chosen_element: The extension tower K/F with Galois group Gal(K/F) ≅ G
AFWD.justification: Forward morphism: the construction of a field extension that realizes G. This is directed ascent from base field F toward the splitting field K, carrying the group structure forward.

AREV.chosen_element: The Galois correspondence: descent from extension K back to fixed field F^G
AREV.justification: Reverse morphism: the contravariant map from subgroups H ⊆ G to intermediate fields F ⊆ K^H ⊆ K. This is the descent/coagula — unwinding the extension to recover the fixed field.

CLINK.chosen_element: Composition of Galois automorphisms: σ ∘ τ for σ, τ ∈ Gal(K/F)
CLINK.justification: Sequential chaining of group elements. The group operation itself is composition of field automorphisms. This is the root→derived form: base automorphisms compose into derived automorphisms.

IMSCRIB.justification: Self-reference: the element that fixes every element of K. It is the stone that knows itself — the automorphism that recognizes the extension as itself.

FSPLIT.chosen_element: Splitting of G into two subgroups H and G/H via a normal subgroup N ⊲ G
FSPLIT.justification: Co-multiplication: one group branches into a subgroup H and its quotient G/H. This corresponds to the intermediate field K^H between F and K. The extension splits into two layers.

FFUSE.chosen_element: Reconstitution of G from H and G/H via the extension sequence F ⊆ K^H ⊆ K
FFUSE.justification: Multiplication: the two branches (subgroup and quotient) fuse back into the original group G. The intermediate field K^H mediates the fusion. FFUSE(FSPLIT(G)) = G by the fundamental theorem of Galois theory.

EVALT.chosen_element: Solvability of G by radicals: G is solvable ⟺ K is constructible by nested radical extensions
EVALT.justification: Affirmative branch: the positive evaluation that the group admits a radical solution. This is the T-state — success in realizing the extension via radicals.

EVALF.chosen_element: Non-solvability of G: G is not solvable ⟺ K cannot be expressed by radicals alone
EVALF.justification: Negative branch: the F-state where radical methods fail. This is the descent into impossibility — the group structure forbids radical construction (e.g., S₅ for quintic equations).

ENGAGR.chosen_element: The paradice of the Inverse Galois Problem: G is simultaneously realizable and unrealizable over different base fields
ENGAGR.justification: Both states held without resolution: a finite group G may be realizable as Gal(K/ℚ) but not as Gal(K/ℚ(√2)), or realizable over ℚ but not over ℚ_p for some prime p. The group exists in a bicameral state — both yes and no, depending on context.

IFIX.chosen_element: The explicit construction of K/F with Gal(K/F) ≅ G — a permanent record in the literature
IFIX.justification: ROM fixation: once a field extension realizing G is constructed and published, it becomes an irreversible, append-only fact. The realization is fixed in the mathematical record.

phase_2.split_element: FSPLIT: normal subgroup N ⊲ G induces intermediate field K^N
split_outputs: Subgroup H = Gal(K/K^N) (stabilizer of the intermediate field)
Quotient G/N = Gal(K^N/F) (Galois group of the intermediate extension)
phase_2.fuse_element: FFUSE: the extension sequence F ⊆ K^N ⊆ K reconstitutes G
phase_2.fuse_result: Gal(K/F) ≅ G (the original group is recovered exactly)

phase_3.void_description: 00 — The abstract finite group G before any field extension is constructed. No realization exists; the group is a pure algebraic object without instantiation.
phase_3.true_description: 01 — The group G is successfully realized as Gal(K/F) for some explicit field extension K/F. The extension is constructible and the Galois correspondence holds.
phase_3.false_description: 10 — The group G cannot be realized as a Galois group over the chosen base field (e.g., S₅ over ℚ by radicals). The extension is impossible or requires transcendental methods.
phase_3.both_description: 11 — The paradice: G is simultaneously realizable and unrealizable depending on the base field, the characteristic, or the context. The group exists in a superposition of success and failure.

steps.domain_action: The abstract finite group G emerges from void — no field extension yet exists.
The algebraic closure ℚ̄ (or base field F) is established as the hermetic boundary containing all possible extensions.
Construct a field extension K/F such that Gal(K/F) ≅ G — forward morphism toward realization.
Compose Galois automorphisms σ, τ ∈ Gal(K/F) to generate the full group structure via σ ∘ τ.
The identity automorphism id_K is recognized as the self-referential element fixing all of K.
A normal subgroup N ⊲ G induces a split into H = Gal(K/K^N) and quotient G/N = Gal(K^N/F).
If G is solvable, the T-branch activates: K is constructible by nested radical extensions.
The Galois correspondence descends from K to the fixed field K^N via the contravariant map.
If G is not solvable (e.g., S₅), the F-branch activates: K cannot be expressed by radicals alone.
The intermediate field K^N mediates the fusion of H and G/N back into G = Gal(K/F).
The paradice emerges: G is simultaneously realizable over ℚ and unrealizable over ℚ(√2), or realizable over ℚ but not over ℚ_p.
The explicit construction of K/F with Gal(K/F) ≅ G is recorded permanently in the mathematical literature.
Further compositions of automorphisms generate all elements of G from generators.
The inverse Galois correspondence maps subgroups back to intermediate fields, confirming the bijection.
A second normal subgroup N' ⊲ G induces a further split into H' and G/N'.
If the quotient G/N' is solvable, the T-branch extends: the intermediate extension K^N'/F is radical.
The second intermediate field K^N' fuses with H' to reconstitute the layer above K^N.
Each intermediate field and its Galois group are recorded as permanent facts.
The full tower F ⊆ K^N' ⊆ K^N ⊆ K enters a paradice: each layer is simultaneously solvable and unsolvable depending on the choice of base field.
The identity automorphism is recognized at every level of the tower, confirming self-reference.
The algebraic closure ℚ̄ remains the terminal anchor, containing all realizations.
The complete Galois correspondence is established: every subgroup of G corresponds to exactly one intermediate field.
The composition of all automorphisms generates the full group G, closing the cycle.
The Inverse Galois Theorem itself — the statement that every finite group is realizable — is fixed as an irreversible mathematical truth.

phase_5.compiler_frontend: The Galois correspondence: it translates the abstract group structure G into the concrete field extension K/F via the bijection between subgroups and intermediate fields.
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Automorphism composition: σ ∘ τ for σ, τ ∈ Gal(K/F). Elements of the group communicate by composing their actions on the field.
phase_5.memory_mechanism: The field extension K itself stores the state. Each element α ∈ K is a memory cell; the Galois group acts on K as the state-transformation engine.
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: The tower of intermediate fields F ⊆ K^N ⊆ K determines the order of operations. Subgroups are processed in reverse order of containment (largest first).
phase_5.alfs_store: The base field F and the algebraic closure ℚ̄ (or the chosen base field). These are the canonical reference stores from which all extensions are bootstrapped.

phase_6.cycle_cost: Each automorphism composition σ ∘ τ costs one group operation. Each FSPLIT/FFUSE pair costs the computation of the intermediate field K^N. Each IFIX costs one permanent record entry.
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: The abstract group G exists in void (00). No field extension has been constructed. No automorphisms have been composed.
phase_6.post_cycle_state: The field extension K/F is realized with Gal(K/F) ≅ G. All automorphisms have been composed and recorded. The Galois correspondence is established. The group structure is fixed in the mathematical record.
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — The Inverse Galois Theorem is entropy-conservative because the realization of G as Gal(K/F) is a bijective correspondence: every finite group maps to exactly one equivalence class of field extensions, and every extension maps back to its Galois group. Information is neither created nor destroyed, only reorganized from abstract group structure into concrete field automorphisms. The cycle VINIT → AFWD → CLINK → FSPLIT → FFUSE → IFIX → IMSCRIB closes without loss or gain of information.

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → TANCH → AFWD → CLINK → IMSCRIB → FSPLIT → EVALT → AREV → EVALF → FFUSE → ENGAGR → IFIX → CLINK → AREV → FSPLIT → EVALT → FFUSE → IFIX → ENGAGR → IMSCRIB → TANCH → AREV → CLINK → IFIX
-- Class: Inverse Galois Theorem
-- Fingerprint: sig=(12,4,5,3)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=3
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=24
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(5, 9), (14, 16)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑡  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑼 → 𐑾  | terminal object — connectivity boundary
--   [2] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑡 → 𐑱  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [3] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑾 → 𐑠  | composition — regime coherence
--   [4] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑱 → 𐑚  | identity — self-imscription
--   [5] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [6] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [7] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑚 → 𐑙  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [8] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [9] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑳  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [10] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑙 → 𐑭  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [11] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑳 → 𐑱  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [12] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑭 → 𐑗  | composition — regime coherence
--   [13] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑱 → 𐑚  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [14] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [15] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [16] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑭  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [17] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑙 → 𐑳  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [18] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑭 → 𐑠  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [19] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑳 → 𐑡  | identity — self-imscription
--   [20] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑠 → 𐑗  | terminal object — connectivity boundary
--   [21] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑡 → 𐑱  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [22] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑗 → 𐑭  | composition — regime coherence
--   [23] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑱 → 𐑼  | irreversible fixation — winding number

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑡)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (The abstract finite group G emerges from void — no field extension yet exists.)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑼 𐑾)  -- [1] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (The algebraic closure ℚ̄ (or base field F) is established as the hermetic bou...)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑡 𐑱)  -- [2] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Construct a field extension K/F such that Gal(K/F) ≅ G — forward morphism tow...)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑾 𐑠)  -- [3] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Compose Galois automorphisms σ, τ ∈ Gal(K/F) to generate the full group struc...)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑱 𐑚)  -- [4] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (The identity automorphism id_K is recognized as the self-referential element ...)
  -- FSPLIT [5] (gran := 𐑚) (A normal subgroup N ⊲ G induces a split into H = Gal(K/K^N) and quotient G/N = Gal(K^N/F).) / FFUSE [9] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (2 nodes)
      .seq

    -- reconnect at FFUSE [9]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑳)  -- [9] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (The intermediate field K^N mediates the fusion of H and G/N back into G = Gal(K/F).)
  (.arrow 𐑳 𐑙 𐑭)  -- [10] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (The paradice emerges: G is simultaneously realizable over ℚ and unrealizable ...)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑳 𐑱)  -- [11] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (The explicit construction of K/F with Gal(K/F) ≅ G is recorded permanently in...)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑭 𐑗)  -- [12] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Further compositions of automorphisms generate all elements of G from generat...)
  (.arrow 𐑗 𐑱 𐑚)  -- [13] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (The inverse Galois correspondence maps subgroups back to intermediate fields,...)
  -- FSPLIT [14] (gran := 𐑚) (A second normal subgroup N' ⊲ G induces a further split into H' and G/N'.) / FFUSE [16] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (1 nodes)
      (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [15] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (If the quotient G/N' is solvable, the T-branch extends: the intermediate exte...)
      -- F-branch (0 nodes)
      (.refl 𐑙))  -- F-branch: empty arc (direct to FFUSE.F)
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [16]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑭)  -- [16] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (The second intermediate field K^N' fuses with H' to reconstitute the layer above K^N.)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑙 𐑳)  -- [17] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (Each intermediate field and its Galois group are recorded as permanent facts.)
  (.arrow 𐑳 𐑭 𐑠)  -- [18] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (The full tower F ⊆ K^N' ⊆ K^N ⊆ K enters a paradice: each layer is simultaneo...)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑳 𐑡)  -- [19] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (The identity automorphism is recognized at every level of the tower, confirmi...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑠 𐑗)  -- [20] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (The algebraic closure ℚ̄ remains the terminal anchor, containing all realizat...)
  (.arrow 𐑗 𐑡 𐑱)  -- [21] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (The complete Galois correspondence is established: every subgroup of G corres...)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑗 𐑭)  -- [22] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (The composition of all automorphisms generates the full group G, closing the ...)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑱 𐑼)  -- [23] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (The Inverse Galois Theorem itself — the statement that every finite group is ...)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def inverse_galois_theorem_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑭 :=
  (inverse_galois_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def inverse_galois_theorem_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑭 :=
  (inverse_galois_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem inverse_galois_theorem_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

end Imscribing
